# Source Card: Recommended Actions Based on Blood Lead Level

## Summary
This CDC clinical-guidance page (Childhood Lead Poisoning Prevention, National Center for Environmental Health, page dated Aug. 21, 2025) defines the childhood Blood Lead Reference Value (BLRV) of 3.5 µg/dL and the graded action tiers keyed to blood lead level (BLL) in µg/dL. It establishes that a capillary screen at or above the BLRV must be confirmed by a venous sample, provides confirmation-timeline and follow-up-testing schedules (Tables 1-2), and directs iron-deficiency testing/treatment (per AAP) for confirmed BLLs in the 3.5-19 µg/dL band. It backfills exact-passage locators for anemia-KB evidence id CDC2025_LEAD.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) BLRV is 3.5 µg/dL; BLL ≥ 3.5 µg/dL triggers the action pathway — locator: heading "If the patient's BLL is ≥ 3.5 micrograms per deciliter" — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:6ded6f4d78060a24a7e437b5f43985f44911e45748b7c98f397d8208cc64c48f]"
- (ev_002) Capillary result ≥ BLRV requires a venous confirmatory sample — locator: "Initial screening blood lead level" first paragraph — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:e14f1f0aa53c8a4ccb12f827570d11ab7726a1945ba3647294d692e6b83d19f4]"
- (ev_003) Confirmation timelines shorten as BLL rises (Table 1): ≥3.5-9 within 3 months, 10-19 within 1 month, 20-44 within 2 weeks, ≥45 within 48 hours — locator: Table 1
- (ev_004) BLL 3.5-19 µg/dL: test for and treat iron deficiency per AAP — locator: "If the patient's BLL is 3.5-19 micrograms per deciliter" — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:11e53d4e61c49223a863efd4dae0d9423d1ca67bcc64c61d196476e4108760f3]"
- (ev_005) Urgency tiers at 20-44 µg/dL and ≥45 µg/dL — locator: two headings "If the patient's BLL is 20-44 micrograms per deciliter" / "If the patient's BLL is ≥ 45 micrograms per deciliter"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: children (childhood lead poisoning prevention), both sexes; screening cadence references Medicaid testing at 12 and 24 months (or 24-72 months if never screened) and risk-based screening otherwise.
- Assay/method dependence: BLL measured in µg/dL; the pathway is explicitly method-aware — capillary (finger/heel-prick) screening vs venous confirmatory draw; capillary result at/above the BLRV mandates a venous sample. Iron-deficiency cutoffs are deferred to AAP guidelines and are NOT numerically specified in this CDC document.
- Lifecycle: page dated Aug. 21, 2025; version = "CDC clinical guidance updated Aug. 21, 2025". BLRV of 3.5 µg/dL reflects CDC's 2021 update (population-based 97.5th percentile) as carried forward on this page; no retire date stated.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Yes. Defines referral/escalation triggers used as anemia-KB safety alerts: BLL 20-44 µg/dL → PEHSU / Poison Control (1-800-222-1222), environmental investigation, complete history/physical (backs KB ALERT-008); BLL ≥45 µg/dL → highest action tier, detailed neurological exam, possible hospital admission, medical-toxicologist consult for gastrointestinal decontamination / chelation (backs KB ALERT-007). Confirmation of an elevated capillary screen via venous draw is itself a defined next-step trigger.

## Limitations & conflicts
- The live cdc.gov URL returned HTTP 403 to automated fetch on 2026-07-18; verbatim passages were captured from the Internet Archive Wayback snapshot (2026-06-29) of the same URL, which mirrors the "Aug. 21, 2025" page. Content is authentic CDC text; a human should re-verify against the live page if it becomes reachable.
- Iron-deficiency numeric thresholds (e.g., ferritin/Hb cutoffs) are NOT locatable in this source — CDC defers to AAP guidelines. GAP: any specific iron-deficiency lab cutoff must be sourced from AAP, not from CDC2025_LEAD.
- Tables 1 and 2 in the mirrored HTML lack in-cell delimiters; row-to-value pairings were reconstructed from row order. The BLL bands and timelines quoted are verbatim, but a human should confirm the Table 2 later-follow-up column pairings against the live table layout.
- No conflicts with other anemia-KB sources recorded. The 3.5 µg/dL BLRV is a reference value (statistical, population-based), not a diagnostic/toxic threshold — do not collapse it with clinical intervention levels.
